title: Post-build close-the-loop is deterministic-only: new MCP feature gets rebuild --changed but no curated enrichment, left as a manual expensive --recouncil
evidence: [AskUserQuestion option:] "Re-council the new MCP feature — Run the dummyindex council (`--recouncil`) on the just-built `core/mcp` area to enrich its `.context/features/` docs (spec/plan/concerns) beyond the deterministic `rebuild --changed` already done. Costs several subagent invocations." ... build check: [x] #28 `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` run ... context rebuild: 23 added, 3 modified, 5 removed
what_happened: The build's final checklist step only runs `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` (deterministic backbone). The newly built core/mcp feature therefore has no curated spec/plan/concerns docs in .context/features/ unless the user separately pays for a full --recouncil — which was offered as an option at session end but never run. Also `.context/_enrich_plan.json` (an internal working artifact) appeared in git status as a tracked add.
root_cause_guess: v0.15.0 predates the reconcile/reconcile-stamp loop (added v0.15.3+); the build skill's closing step was rebuild-only by design, leaving curated taxonomy stale for new features. The session is evidence that prompted the later commit-anchored reconcile work.

title: Proposal cites symbols.json grep line numbers as if they were source-file locations
evidence: Grounding cmd output: "=== key symbols (path:line) === | 358: \"name\": \"_build_owner_filter\", | ... | 5870: \"name\": \"ContainerService\"," → plan.md: "6. **Remove containers.** Delete `app/core/workspaces/containers.py` (`ContainerService` :5870)" and summary: "`ContainerService` (`workspaces/containers.py:5870`, delete), `OwnerRef`/`owner_fks` (`resources/models.py:5272/5298`...)"
what_happened: Following the skill instruction to 'cite reused symbols from .context/map/symbols.json', Claude grepped the monolithic symbols.json and harvested grep's line numbers (358, 956, 969, 5272, 5298, 5870, 6000 — offsets inside the JSON file) then wrote them into spec.md/plan.md as if they were source-file line numbers (containers.py is nowhere near 5870 lines). Build agents executing this checklist would chase bogus locations. Repeated across ~8 symbol citations in the written proposal.
root_cause_guess: symbols.json is one giant JSON blob and the skill gives no retrieval verb (e.g. `dummyindex query symbol <name>` returning path:line), so the model improvises grep and confuses JSON offsets with source locations. Provide a symbol-lookup command or embed path:line next to each name in the docs the skill tells the model to read.

title: SessionStart drift report ignored; session ended without rebuild/reconcile despite overlapping feature edits
evidence: SessionStart hook: "## .context/ drift report\n\n4 source files across 4 features have been edited since the matching `.context/features/<id>/` docs were last touched. ... - **app-infra-tools** — app/main.py - **auth-enums** — app/config.py - **integrations-oauth** — app/config.py - **utils-config-fragment** — app/config.py"
what_happened: The drift hook fired correctly at session start (exit 0, 1.7s) and flagged integrations-oauth among 4 drifted features. The session then spent hours rewriting integration-ownership semantics across .context/docs/* and committed, but never updated the drifted feature docs, never ran `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` or any reconcile, and deferred reindexing to Task 16 of a future code migration. The report is advisory-only ('If your current task overlaps any of these features, review and update...') and nothing later in the session re-surfaces it, so the drift persisted across the whole session and the new target-state docs now also diverge from `.context/features/*` (still INFERRED with containers).
root_cause_guess: Drift report is a one-shot SessionStart suggestion with no in-session re-check or Stop-time gate (the auto-council drift gate was built only after this session, 2026-06-11); easy for a long session to scroll it out of context.

title: Curated .context frontmatter owner silently changed @karim → @ahmed by something other than the session
evidence: git diff --cached .context/conventions/folder-organization.md: "-owner: '@karim' | +owner: '@ahmed'" ... ASSISTANT: "plus three I didn't author — an `owner: @karim→@ahmed` rename in `folder-organization.md`, a generated `bos-domain-model-graph.html`, and the pre-existing `mcp-design-tokens-from-db/spec.md` edit"
what_happened: When committing, the assistant found already-staged modifications it had not made, including an owner-frontmatter rename on a curated dummyindex conventions file (whose sibling docs all say owner: '@karim'). It flagged them but committed them anyway as part of the WIP checkpoint, so the unexplained metadata change is now in history. Single occurrence.
root_cause_guess: Most plausibly a prior dummyindex rebuild/ingest run stamping the frontmatter owner from the current git user, clobbering the curated value (confidence low — could also be a manual edit in an earlier session); worth checking whether deterministic rebuild rewrites owner fields on curated taxonomy files.

title: Deterministic backbone over-segmented 1022 files into 734 communities; documented council would cost ~1000+ agent dispatches
evidence: The deterministic backbone shattered this 1022-file repo into **734 Leiden communities** (650 trivial, 84 "non-trivial" ...). Yet the actual product structure is only **6 feature slices** ... Following the documented council *strictly* means ~84 per-feature councils + **650 individual trivial-consolidation agent passes** ... ≈ **1000+ agent dispatches** — wildly out of proportion with the "standard ≈ $6–10" the onboarding implied (that estimate assumed N≈14, not 734).
what_happened: On this Next.js frontend the graph-community detection produced 734 features for what is really a ~13-feature repo. The documented council pipeline scaled with raw community count, contradicting the cost estimate shown at onboarding. Claude had to stop and ask; the user instructed 'be smart about ti first do a llm council pass and reduce the segments before moving into actual enrichment', and Claude improvised an off-script taxonomy collapse (ad-hoc bucketing scripts, an architect agent, scaffold/remove surgery).
root_cause_guess: Leiden community detection with no post-clustering consolidation pass is unsuited to feature-sliced frontend repos (many small import islands); the council docs and cost model assume N≈14 features and provide no built-in 'reduce segments first' stage.

title: Throwaway scaffold-feature test feature leaked back into the index after non-force removal failed
evidence: error: feature '_zztest' still owns 2 file(s) that exist on disk (e.g. shared/lib/api/fetc... [later] The `_zztest` throwaway leaked back in (my earlier non-force cleanup failed). Let me force-remove it to land on exactly 13 features
what_happened: Claude scaffolded a `_zztest` feature to learn the verb's behavior. Plain features-remove refused because the feature 'owns files that exist on disk' — true of essentially every real feature — so the cleanup silently failed and `_zztest` resurfaced in features/INDEX.json after the big restructure, requiring a second --force removal. The index briefly read 14 features instead of 13.
root_cause_guess: features-remove's no-force guard (refuse if owned files exist on disk) is inverted for the common case; failure exit didn't make the earlier compound command abort, so stale state survived into refresh-indexes.

title: Flow auto-detection is mostly noise: 60 of 75 detected flows discarded (enum-class roots, private helpers, BFS over-reach)
evidence: "workspace-org-core: kept 0, discarded 6. ... **flow-002** (`OrganizationMemberRole`): enum class entry point, 324 steps across 50 files — same pattern" ... "**flow-038** (`ExecStatus`): Enum class entry point (not callable); trace exploded to 100+ steps across 37 unrelated files (breadth mis-" ... "**flow-066** (`build_version_snapshot`): Misdetected breadth — the trace is a reverse call-graph showing which asset/doc service methods call this utility, not a forward execution sequence." Final report: "Flows: 15 kept, 60 discarded (enum-class roots, `_`-private helpers, BFS over-reach)"
what_happened: The deterministic flow extractor treated enum classes and `_`-prefixed private helpers as entry points and produced traces of 100-324 steps across 37-50 unrelated files; at least one trace was a reverse call-graph rather than a forward execution path. Multiple features (ai-chat-streaming, workspace-org-core, app-infra-tools) discarded ALL 6 of their flows. 11 flow-refinement subagent dispatches were spent largely refuting deterministic noise (80% discard rate).
root_cause_guess: Entry-point heuristic doesn't filter non-callable symbols (Enum classes) or private names, and the trace builder does unbounded BFS over the call graph (sometimes following reverse edges) with no breadth/depth cutoff.

title: Entry-point detection misses real FastAPI HTTP handlers, leaving 5 of 11 features with zero flows
evidence: "Caveat: 5 of 11 features kept **0** flows because AST entry-point detection missed the real HTTP handlers (`chat()`, `accept_invitation()`, …) — behaviour for those lives in their `spec.md`/`plan.md`, not `flows/`"
what_happened: The flip side of the flow-noise problem: while inventing flows from enums, the AST extractor failed to detect the decorated FastAPI route handlers that are the actual entry points of this backend, so nearly half the features shipped with no flow narratives at all — a permanent coverage gap in the committed index acknowledged in the final report.
root_cause_guess: Entry-point detection doesn't recognize framework decorators (@router.get/post etc.) as roots; it likely keys off naming/call-graph in-degree only, which misses decorator-registered handlers.

title: Repo's canonical docs in .context/docs/ self-exclude from the source-docs catalog, even when passed explicitly via --docs
evidence: [after `dummyindex ingest ... --docs .../backend/.context/docs --docs .../BOS-Mono/.context/docs`] "backend canon docs: 0" ... Final report: "Note: the backend's own `.context/docs/` canon self-excludes from the catalog — I compensated by injecting explicit canon pointers (CONVENTIONS/DECISIONS/SCHEMA) into every dispatch, which worked"
what_happened: The team keeps its canon (ARCHITECTURE.md, CONVENTIONS.md, DECISIONS.md, SCHEMA.md, BACKLOG.md) in backend/.context/docs/. First ingest auto-discovered only 2 docs (CLAUDE.md, README.md). Re-ingest with the directory passed explicitly via --docs still catalogued 0 backend canon docs (the mono-root's 246 docs were catalogued fine) because the scanner blanket-excludes .context/ as its own output dir. The orchestrator had to hand-inject canon pointers into all ~56 subagent prompts — grounding that future sessions navigating only via the index will NOT get.
root_cause_guess: The ingest doc scanner excludes the .context/ directory wholesale (to avoid indexing its own output) and the exclusion is applied even to explicitly user-supplied --docs roots that happen to live under a .context/ path.

title: Graph partitioner leaks a parser-artifact rationale node into its own single-node community (spurious feature)
evidence: "**Outcome: C — keep as standalone with parser-artifact note. Builder-bug leak recorded.** Evidence: `config_rationale_169` is a `file_type: rationale` node (the `_split_origins` docstring at `app/config.py` L169). The graph partitioner placed it alone into community 14 while every real `config.py` symbol ... landed in communities 1 and 4 ... The node has no edges, no call sites, and no entry points: it was never a real feature member. The `_is_parser_artifact` filter should have dropped `file_type: rationale` nodes before community assignment; one leaked through."
what_happened: Leiden community detection produced a junk feature (`utils-config-fragment`) consisting of a single orphaned docstring-rationale node. It survived the trivial filter into the council, costing a dedicated architect consolidation dispatch (~36k subagent tokens) just to diagnose it as a builder bug, and ends up as a permanent parser-artifact stub feature in the committed index.
root_cause_guess: The `_is_parser_artifact` pre-filter runs incompletely or after edge construction; `file_type: rationale` nodes with zero edges aren't dropped before community assignment, so an isolated node forms its own community.

title: Onboarding runs after the first full ingest, so external doc roots require a second full re-ingest
evidence: [first ingest] "context init: wrote 218 files ... files: 151" → [source-docs after it] "total docs catalogued: 2\n- CLAUDE.md ... - README.md" → [after onboarding answers] "dummyindex ingest /mnt/.../backend --docs .../backend/.context/docs --docs .../BOS-Mono/.context/docs" → "AST extraction: 151/151 files (100%) | context init: wrote 218 files"
what_happened: The skill's pipeline ingests first, then asks the onboarding questions — including 'any prose-doc roots outside this repo?'. The user's answer (mono-root docs) was persisted to config.json via `context onboard`, but applying it meant re-running the entire ingest with explicit --docs flags (full 151-file AST re-extraction and 218-file rewrite). ingest apparently doesn't read external_docs from the just-written config.json either, since the flags had to be passed manually.
root_cause_guess: Phase ordering in the skill (ingest before onboarding) plus ingest not consuming .context/config.json's external_docs; doc cataloguing isn't an incremental step separable from the full ingest.

title: Committed generated .context/ artifacts cause merge conflicts on every rebase (3 of 4 commits conflicted)
evidence:
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .context/meta.json
error: could not apply fba707a... chore: update indexed_commit and updated_at in meta.json
[next commit]
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .context/conventions/naming.md
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .context/features/INDEX.json
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .context/features/INDEX.md
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .context/features/app-infra-tools/spec.md
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .context/features/symbol-graph.json
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .context/source-docs/INDEX.json
what_happened: A routine `git rebase origin/development` conflicted on 3 of 4 replayed commits — every conflict was in generated `.context/` files (timestamps, file counts, symbol snapshots, generated-at lines). Claude resolved all with `checkout --ours` and regenerated afterward, but this consumed the first ~25% of the session and is structural: dummyindex both mandates committing the index ('dedicated commits') and regenerates volatile per-machine content into tracked files.
root_cause_guess: Volatile fields (updated_at, indexed_commit, '_Generated <timestamp>_' lines, counts) live inside tracked artifacts with no merge driver/.gitattributes strategy and no 'regenerate on conflict' guidance; concurrent sessions on diverged branches inevitably collide.

title: meta.json dummyindex_version frozen at 0.15.0 — non-destructive rebuild path never re-stamps it; fixed by hand-editing
evidence:
.context/meta.json:6:  "dummyindex_version": "0.15.0",
[user]: "no it should all show the current version"
[assistant]: "the `dummyindex_version` field only re-stamps on a *destructive* full rebuild (which re-clusters and wipes curated features — never run here). The non-destructive `rebuild --changed` path deliberately preserves it, so it had been frozen at its original `0.15.0` creation stamp."
what_happened: After upgrading everything to 0.25.0, the user asked whether everything was up to date; meta.json still said 0.15.0 and the user pushed back ('no it should all show the current version'). Claude traced rebuild.py/incremental.py to confirm only a destructive --full re-cluster re-stamps the version, then hand-edited meta.json and verified the edit survives refresh-indexes and rebuild --changed.
root_cause_guess: The version stamp is treated as a creation-time field; the safe incremental path intentionally preserves all of meta.json instead of updating the tool-version field, leaving no non-destructive way to record which version maintains the index.

title: tree.json never enriched: all 1117 nodes still deterministic stubs from the original ingest
evidence:
context enrich-plan: wrote .context/cache/_enrich_plan.json
  total nodes: 1117  stubs: 1117  by_kind: {'project': 1, 'dir': 48, 'file': 176, 'class': 49, 'method': 257, 'function': 586}
  batches: 177
[assistant]: "All 1117 tree nodes are stubs — `tree.json` was never enriched in the original run. That's a **whole-repo, pre-existing gap** (177 file-batches)"
what_happened: During the reconcile, Claude discovered the PageIndex-style tree.json — the artifact future sessions navigate by — was 100% deterministic stubs (1117/1117 nodes), meaning the original full ingest never completed (or never ran) Phase 4.5 tree enrichment. It was flagged as out-of-scope and left unfixed, so retrieval over the tree still reads auto-generated stubs, not prose.
root_cause_guess: The original ingest either skipped tree-enrich silently or was interrupted with no completion check; nothing in status/preflight/drift reporting surfaces 'tree enrichment 0% complete', so the gap persisted invisibly across versions 0.15→0.25.

title: rebuild --changed on CLI 0.25.0 re-shatters curated 13-feature taxonomy into 838 community-N clusters
evidence: "⚠️ **Stop — something went wrong.** The pre-commit's `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` (now on 0.25.0) appears to have **re-shattered the curated 13-feature taxonomy into hundreds of `community-N` clusters**" ... later controlled retest: "=== run rebuild --changed (stamp now 0.25.0 — does it re-shatter?) === context rebuild: 0 added, 5 modified, 0 removed → rebuilt ... community dirs created: 838 / curated features intact: 13/13 / untracked paths: 838"
what_happened: A pre-commit hook wired to `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` (per user request to auto-update .context) polluted the working tree with 838 untracked .context/features/community-N/ dirs and rewrote 17 tracked .context files mid-commit, aborting the commit. Under 0.24.0 the same command had printed "enriched index preserved ... no re-cluster" (safe). The agent had to `git clean -fd .context/` + `git checkout HEAD -- .context/` to recover, then deliberately re-ran the command as a test on the now-0.25.0-stamped index and it re-shattered AGAIN (proving the regression is version-of-CLI, not index-stamp). All rebuild automation (Stop hook + pre-commit) was then reverted as unsafe. Occurred twice (incident + retest).
root_cause_guess: 0.25.0 regression: rebuild --changed no longer takes the preserve-enriched/no-recluster path on a curated index — it runs full community clustering (likely the curated-index detection or migration path broke between 0.24.0 and 0.25.0), writing raw community-N feature dirs alongside the curated taxonomy.

title: Committed .context generated artifacts cause repeated rebase merge conflicts and massive churn
evidence: "CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .context/features/brand-hub/feature.json ... error: could not apply 7965b456... docs(context): reconcile illustrations into brand-hub + tests features" ... "[detached HEAD d6e9c6e5] chore(context): refresh deterministic backbone after rebase onto development  10 files changed, 36137 insertions(+), 34837 deletions(-)"
what_happened: A 9-commit rebase onto origin/development conflicted 3 separate times, exclusively on dummyindex-generated files: feature.json (twice, requiring 8 manual union-merge edits of generated JSON member/file arrays), plus 7 deterministic backbone files (naming.md, INDEX.json, symbol-graph.json, map/*.json, meta.json) where hand-merging was pointless and the agent took --theirs then regenerated. Backbone refresh commits run to ~36k changed lines each, guaranteeing conflicts on any parallel branch.
root_cause_guess: dummyindex's workflow commits huge order-sensitive generated JSON artifacts into git with no merge driver, no union strategy (.gitattributes), and no 'regenerate instead of merge' guidance — so any two branches that both reconcile will conflict.

title: feature.json confidence semantics are opaque — rebuild resets councilled features to EXTRACTED, enrichment signal hides in markdown headers
evidence: "Now it's clear: **every** feature in this repo carries `feature.json confidence: EXTRACTED` — including fully-councilled ones (chat, auth, brain) ... The enrichment signal lives in the markdown headers (`spec.md: confidence: INFERRED`), not `feature.json`. So `EXTRACTED` is the correct normal state."
what_happened: After rebuild --changed, all four re-enriched features showed confidence EXTRACTED in feature.json, prompting the user to ask "was there a recouncil?" and then demand "i need .context to be up to date with llm coucil and everything done to date". The agent spent multiple tool calls reverse-engineering that feature.json EXTRACTED is normal even post-council and the real enrichment flag is a `confidence: INFERRED` header inside spec.md/plan.md — two parallel confidence systems with different meanings.
root_cause_guess: Dual confidence representations (feature.json/INDEX.json enum vs markdown front-line) that rebuild and council update independently; no status verb reports enrichment state coherently, so both user and agent doubt whether the council ran.

title: Plan drafted wrong facts about existing code despite .context grounding; only the critique panel caught them
evidence: Critic: "Several findings turn on facts the plan got wrong about the existing code ... `list_for_brand` (reads.py:42-48) currently has signature `brand_id, kind, requesting_user_id` — **no `*args`/`conn` param, and it acquires its own connection internally**. ... the byte-identical claim hinges on reproducing that exact whitespace."
what_happened: The spec/plan, written while navigating .context features/conventions/symbols, asserted incorrect signatures and SQL-construction details for the code it planned to modify. The testability critic (reading raw source) corrected them and the plan was revised. The safety net worked, but the index-first drafting produced wrong load-bearing facts — suggesting symbols.json/feature docs lack signature-level fidelity or the skill under-emphasizes verifying against raw source before drafting.
root_cause_guess: The deterministic map (symbols.json) records names/locations but not signatures/string-literal details, and the plan skill encourages drafting from the index without a verify-against-source step.

title: `rebuild --changed` never adds NEW files to the backbone, and scaffold-feature/assign-files don't register them either — required hand-editing .context/cache/manifest.json
evidence:
context rebuild: no source files changed; .context/ unchanged.
[...] `rebuild --changed` re-extracts *modified known* files but doesn't **add** new ones — the new files are absent from the backbone. [...workaround:] cp .context/cache/manifest.json /tmp/dummyindex_manifest_backup.json ... 'files': {k:v for k,v in m['files'].items() if k not in drop} ... manifest files: 191 -> 184
=== rebuild --changed === AST extraction: 189/189 files (100%)
what_happened: During reconcile, 7 genuinely new source files (guideline_roles package, conversion.py, new tests) were reported as unassigned_new_files but no documented CLI path could get them AST-extracted into map/files.json: `rebuild --changed` said "no source files changed", and after `scaffold-feature`/`assign-files` the files were still OUT of the backbone (verified twice). Claude's workaround was to delete the 7 entries from the internal cache file .context/cache/manifest.json by hand so the next `rebuild --changed` would treat them as changed and extract them. It worked, but it's unsanctioned surgery on internal state that any user-facing flow would get wrong.
root_cause_guess: Change detection is manifest-hash based and only diffs files already present in the manifest; new-file ingestion is only implemented in the full (destructive) rebuild path. Placement verbs update feature.json membership but never touch the AST backbone or manifest.

title: Three mutually contradictory staleness oracles: SessionStart mtime drift report (12 features) vs git-anchored reconcile (6) vs content hashes (8), worsened by an anchor left unstamped by a prior session
evidence: the SessionStart report's 12 was the looser *mtime* signal; the git-anchored 6 is authoritative [...] "No source files changed" — meaning the deterministic backbone was already refreshed previously; only the commit **anchor** lagged (a prior `rebuild --changed` without a `reconcile-stamp`). [...] Decisive result — and it overturns the mtime/batch signal: recomputing **content** hashes shows the 6 git-drifted features are genuinely **STALE**, plus `utils-config-fragment` STALE and `guideline-roles` NO-HASH.
what_happened: The SessionStart hook reported 68 files across 12 drifted features (mtime-based); `reconcile --json` reported 6 (git-anchor-based); .hash mtimes plus council-batch implied 0 of those 6 needed work; only Claude's manual content-hash recomputation established the true set of 8 stale features. Claude flip-flopped twice ('the council already re-ran on them' → 'this overturns the earlier read decisively') before getting it right. A prior session's `rebuild --changed` without `reconcile-stamp` had left the anchor lagging, manufacturing part of the false drift.
root_cause_guess: Each subsystem (hook, reconcile, council resume) uses a different staleness signal with no single authoritative source, and the two-step rebuild/stamp protocol is easy to leave half-done with nothing detecting the desync.

title: build --next-wave keyword matching mis-assigns checklist items to wrong specialists, skills, and hooks
evidence: "index": 2, "text": "`features/brand-hub/lib/illustration-upload.ts` — `uploadIllustration` ...", "agent": "frontend-verify", "subagent_type": "general-purpose", "fallback": false ... "index": 3 ... "agent": "prettier-format", "subagent_type": "general-purpose" ... "index": 4 ... `IllustrationCard.tsx` ... "agent": "frontend-reviewer"  ||  Assistant: "the CLI's keyword matching mis-assigned them ... the CLI mis-matched it to `frontend-reviewer` (a *reviewer*, not an implementer) ... the `general-purpose` fallbacks came from keyword mis-matches to capability specialists."
what_happened: First `dummyindex context build --next-wave --json` call: of 5 Wave-1 items, only 1 was correctly assigned. A design-export prep step was matched to typescript-implementer, two pure TS lib helpers were matched to `frontend-verify` (a generated SKILL) and `prettier-format` (a PostToolUse HOOK) — both un-dispatchable, downgraded to general-purpose with `fallback: false` (misleading metadata) — and a component-implementation item was matched to a reviewer agent. Claude detected the mis-assignments itself and overrode all of them, dispatching typescript-implementer manually. Discussed/repeated at L276 and L316.
root_cause_guess: The wave item→agent matcher is naive keyword matching over the whole equipment manifest (including skills and hooks, not just Task-dispatchable agents), with no role awareness (implementer vs reviewer vs verifier) and a `fallback` flag that doesn't reflect the general-purpose downgrade.

title: rebuild --changed won't ADD new files to the backbone; gitignored manifest gates it; the workaround lives only in a hand-written session memory note
evidence: Memory note read mid-procedure: "**`rebuild --changed` re-extracts MODIFIED known files but does NOT add NEW files** to the backbone (`map/files.json`, `symbols.json`, `tree.json`)... if a path is in the manifest, `--changed` skips it even when the tracked backbone lacks it. **Fix to force new files into the backbone non-destructively:** drop their entries from `manifest.json`, then `rebuild --changed`... A `git checkout -- .context` can desync the tracked backbone (old) from the gitignored manifest (current)."
what_happened: To execute the reconcile correctly, Claude had to consult its own prior-session memory note ('Hard-won mechanics') documenting that the advertised non-destructive incremental rebuild silently skips new files because the gitignored cache/manifest.json gates --changed, plus a manual manifest-realign trick. In this run the backbone happened to already contain the files (manifest stale at 184 vs 222 actual), but the documented CLI path alone cannot fold new files in.
root_cause_guess: --changed change-detection is driven by a local gitignored cache rather than the committed backbone or git itself, so cache/backbone desync (e.g., after git checkout of .context) makes incremental rebuilds skip exactly the files that need indexing. None of this is in the official skill docs.

title: context check keeps reporting 'drift detected — 36 added, 26 modified' even after a complete, verified reconcile
evidence: Post-reconcile verification: "=== content drift vs manifest (context check) === context check: drift detected — 36 added, 26 modified, 0 removed" — Claude: "**`context check`** reads the **gitignored `cache/manifest.json`** (184 entries, local-only cache), which lags disk... A future `rebuild --changed` after commit resolves it."
what_happened: After the full reconcile (all files placed, 12 features re-enriched, anchor stamped, gate clean), `context check` still reported the same 36 added/26 modified drift it reported before the work, because it compares source against the stale gitignored manifest. The signal that originally exposed the real drift is now permanently crying wolf until some future rebuild — indistinguishable from genuine drift.
root_cause_guess: `context check` hashes against cache/manifest.json, but nothing in the reconcile procedure (assign-files, mark-enriched, refresh-indexes, reconcile-stamp) updates that manifest, so check and reconcile can never converge within one procedure.

title: .context/meta.json version stamp stuck at 0.15.0 while CLI and backbone are at 0.24.0
evidence: "=== installed CLI === 0.24.0 === .context stamp === \"dummyindex_version\": \"0.15.0\", \"schema_version\": 1, === pyproject dev dep === \"dummyindex>=0.24\"," — Claude: "the backbone was refreshed under 0.24 (per the #16 commit) but the version stamp in `meta.json` wasn't bumped."
what_happened: Despite a dedicated PR (#16) updating dummyindex to 0.24.0 and refreshing the .context backbone, and multiple rebuild/refresh-indexes runs in this session, meta.json still records dummyindex_version 0.15.0 — making 'which version built this index' unanswerable from the index itself.
root_cause_guess: rebuild --changed / refresh-indexes / install paths update artefacts but never rewrite the dummyindex_version field, which is only set at init/full rebuild.

title: Deterministic flow detection generated bogus flows (flow-011/012) that a council agent had to prune as misdetected
evidence: reconcile --json removed_files: ".context/features/integrations-oauth/flows/flow-011.json ... flow-012.md" — Claude: "the **4 flow files** (`flow-011`, `flow-012` × json/md) the integrations-oauth agent correctly pruned as misdetected."
what_happened: During re-enrichment, the integrations-oauth council agent found two flows in the curated index that did not correspond to real code paths and deleted all four files. The misdetected flows had been sitting in the index serving wrong answers since the original build.
root_cause_guess: The deterministic flow extractor over-detects entry-point chains; nothing validates generated flows against source until a human/agent enrichment pass happens to look.

title: Full `context rebuild` re-clusters the feature layer, orphaning all 14 curated council-authored features
evidence: "`dummyindex context rebuild` (full, no `--changed`) regenerates `map/` + `tree.json` (good — picks up new modules) BUT re-runs graph community-detection from scratch, overwriting `features/INDEX.json` with raw `community-N` clusters and dropping the architect's Phase-2 structural regrouping (`_structural-plan.json` is deleted). The prior curated semantic features ... survive only as **orphaned folders on disk + in git** — they're no longer in INDEX.json." ... away_summary: "I recovered the curated `.context` features after a full rebuild scrambled them"
what_happened: After `rebuild --changed` proved blind to a new 18-file module, the user picked "Full /dummyindex pipeline". The full rebuild wrote 295 files, replaced the 14 curated semantic features in INDEX.json with 17 generic community-0..16 clusters, deleted _structural-plan.json, and also modified curated prose (.context/PROJECT.md, architecture/overview.md, conventions/naming.md). Claude spent most of a 44-minute turn (214 messages) on manual recovery: git checkout of INDEX.json, rm -rf community-*, then hand-rebuilding the feature. Claude wrote a memory note titled full-rebuild-destroys-curated-features.
root_cause_guess: Full rebuild unconditionally re-runs community detection and overwrites features/INDEX.json with raw clusters; it has no merge/preserve path for an existing curated feature roster, no pre-rebuild snapshot, and no warning that the feature layer will be reset.

title: `rebuild --changed` is a no-op while an entire new 18-file module is missing from the map (manifest/map desync)
evidence: "context rebuild: no source files changed; .context/ unchanged." ... "The **manifest** (`.context/cache/manifest.json`) already tracks all 158 `.py` files *including* the new `app/core/mcp/*` module — but `map/files.json` / `map/symbols.json` (dated **Jun 8**) contain **zero** `core/mcp` references. That's why `rebuild --changed` is a no-op: it compares source hashes against the manifest, the manifest already matches, so it concludes \"nothing changed\" — but it never notices the *map itself* is missing the entire MCP module."
what_happened: User ran `/dummyindex rebuild --changed` to index the new app/core/mcp/ MCP-server module. The command reported nothing changed even though the module (18 files) had zero entries in map/files.json. The manifest had somehow been updated (Jun 8 06:36) without the map being regenerated (map dated Jun 8 13:37 but missing the module), so the hash comparison passed. This is the exact command CLAUDE.md and the skill recommend for catching the index up, and it cannot.
root_cause_guess: --changed anchors change detection solely on hash-compare against cache/manifest.json and never validates that manifest-tracked files actually exist in map/files.json; some earlier operation updated the manifest without rebuilding the map, leaving a permanently invisible gap.

title: Full rebuild's file discovery is manifest-seeded: silently skips files newer than the prior manifest (3-file extraction gap survives a full rebuild)
evidence: "app/**/*.py with defs>0 but 0 extracted symbols: 3 ... app/core/integrations/credentials.py src_defs=11 in_files.json=False ... tokens.py: ast.parse OK -> dummyindex extraction bug, not bad syntax" ... "it's a **dummyindex `rebuild` bug**: a full rebuild doesn't index files that are newer than the prior manifest (its file discovery is manifest-seeded, not a fresh walk). The committed map was already missing them; my rebuild didn't fix it and `--changed` can't."
what_happened: Even after the destructive FULL rebuild (176 files / 875 symbols), three Python files with real defs (credentials.py 11 defs, tokens.py 1, _token_formatters.py 3) had zero extracted symbols and were absent from files.json. All three parse cleanly with ast.parse and are exactly the three files missing from the pre-rebuild manifest. This later caused two reality-check false positives ("file not found") against accurate docs.
root_cause_guess: rebuild seeds its file walk from the existing cache/manifest.json instead of doing a fresh filesystem walk, so files absent from the prior manifest are never discovered/extracted even on a full rebuild.

title: Drift report, `--refresh`, and `rebuild --changed` measure three different things; the documented remediation doesn't clear the reported drift
evidence: "## Heads-up: live drift not cleared by refresh ... If you want the `.context/` docs brought in sync with the MCP-server changes, the right command is `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` (or a targeted `--recouncil` ...)" — followed next turn by: "context rebuild: no source files changed; .context/ unchanged." Drift report: "_Drift clears naturally: once you edit a feature doc, its mtime updates and these entries drop off._"
what_happened: The SessionStart drift report (mtime-based, doc vs source) flagged 4 drifted features. `--refresh` (refresh-indexes) doesn't touch drift; Claude then recommended `rebuild --changed` as 'the right command' to clear it — but that is manifest-hash based and reported no changes, leaving the drift untouched. At session end the 4 features were still drifted. Three overlapping freshness mechanisms with incompatible semantics confused even the orchestrating skill.
root_cause_guess: Drift (doc-mtime vs source-mtime), refresh-indexes (regenerate derived indexes), and rebuild --changed (source-hash vs manifest) are independent mechanisms with no shared model; the skill docs conflate them, so the recommended command can never resolve the hook's complaint.

title: Community detection fragments a cohesive new package across 6+ clusters, mixing source and tests
evidence: "The clustering is genuinely fragmented — `community-3` has 8 MCP files (but buried among 26 files dominated by `core/ai/*`), `community-6` has 7 (mostly MCP *tests*), the rest scattered 1–2 each. No single community is a clean MCP feature."
what_happened: Even setting aside the destructiveness, the full rebuild's clustering scattered the 18-file app/core/mcp/ package across at least 6 community clusters (community-0/3/6/10/11...), with tests and unrelated core/ai files interleaved, so no cluster could be renamed into the mcp-server feature. This made the rebuild output unusable as a starting point.
root_cause_guess: Graph community detection over the symbol/import graph ignores package/directory boundaries and test-vs-source separation as clustering priors.

title: Proposal shipped with a factually wrong rationale that dummyindex grounding never caught
evidence: My earlier framing — and the **Python port's own module docstring** (`app/core/mcp/tools/brand.py:30`, "the static token tree comes from `design_tokens()`… not the TS Supabase/proxy layer") — implied the TS *MCP* read tokens from Supabase and the port regressed that. **That's wrong.** The TS MCP tools always read the static file; there was never a DB-backed MCP token path.
what_happened: During the /dummyindex-plan run, Claude wrote spec.md/plan.md framing the change as 'restoring' a DB-backed token path the TS app supposedly had (stated explicitly at the investigation stage: 'The TS MCP server read design tokens from Supabase (brand_themes); the Python port replaced that with the static tokens.json'). The 'consistency-checked' propose flow, the .context mcp-server feature docs, and the deterministic scan all failed to flag this; the wrong premise sat in the durable proposal until the USER asked an unrelated follow-up ('how was the main app ... handling brand-theme?'), after which an Explore agent disproved it and Claude had to patch spec.md and add a 'Prior art' correction. Occurred once but the wrong claim persisted across two turns and a written artifact.
root_cause_guess: dummyindex-plan's consistency scan only greps the local .context index — it has no step to verify factual claims inherited from source docstrings or cross-repo history, so the index/proposal happily encodes an inaccurate 'port regression' narrative seeded by a wrong docstring that the original ingest/council also never flagged in concerns.md.

title: Consistency scan's related_features are mostly noise for the actual feature
evidence: context propose: /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/backend/.context/proposals/mcp-design-tokens-from-db (4 files) related features: mcp-server, docs-app-bootstrap, assets-resources, integrations-oauth, artifacts-providers
what_happened: For a 'design tokens from Supabase for the MCP server' proposal, the deterministic scan returned 5 related features of which 3 (docs-app-bootstrap, integrations-oauth, artifacts-providers) are unrelated to design tokens; Claude implicitly confirmed the noise by reading and grounding only on mcp-server and assets-resources. Harmless here but it dilutes the grounding signal that proposal.json/spec.md Consistency blocks carry into build agents.
root_cause_guess: Keyword/symbol-overlap retrieval in `context propose` — the title term 'tokens' likely matched OAuth token handling (integrations-oauth) and shared config/bootstrap symbols, with no relevance ranking or threshold before the hits are written into proposal.json.

title: mtime-based drift report disagrees with commit-anchored reconcile; only clearable by manually `touch`-ing .context docs (workaround used twice)
evidence: "The commit-anchored reconcile is clean (`has_drift: False`) — that's the authoritative signal. The remaining mtime hints (brain/chat/shared-lib/shared-ui-custom) are the trivial barrel/nav edits I already verified are spec-irrelevant, surfacing only because git checkouts left source mtimes newer tha[n]…" — followed by: "# Touch the reviewed feature docs to clear mtime drift … for f in brain chat shared-lib shared-ui-custom; do touch .context/features/$f/spec.md .context/features/$f/plan.md"
what_happened: After reconcile-stamp reported has_drift: False, the SessionStart-hook view (plan-update) still flagged 6 files across 4 features because git checkout/rebase reset source mtimes. Claude had to manually `touch` spec.md/plan.md files to silence it — done again later for brand-hub after the second rebase. False-positive drift that survives a clean stamp and demands a filesystem hack.
root_cause_guess: plan-update compares filesystem mtimes, which git operations churn arbitrarily; it isn't reconciled with (or suppressed by) the authoritative commit-anchored stamp, and there is no sanctioned 'mark reviewed' verb — touch is the only escape.

title: `rebuild --changed` leaves features/INDEX.json + INDEX.md counts stale; Claude hand-edited generated index files
evidence: "`members` already agree at 348, but `file_count` disagrees: `feature.json` correctly lists 199 files (development's 185 + my 14 illustrations), while `INDEX.json`/`INDEX.md` still show development's stale 185. `feature.json` is the source of truth. Let me sync the two index summaries to match:"
what_happened: After resolving rebase conflicts and running `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`, the per-feature roll-up counts in .context/features/INDEX.json and INDEX.md were not regenerated to match feature.json, so Claude manually Edit-patched two generated files (file_count 185→199). Only much later in the session did it discover/use `refresh-indexes`, which does regenerate them — the rebuild path and the documented reconcile procedure didn't include it at that point.
root_cause_guess: rebuild --changed refreshes only a subset of deterministic artifacts (map/, symbol-graph, meta, source-docs) and does not re-derive feature INDEX summaries from feature.json; procedure docs don't say refresh-indexes is required after manual feature.json edits.

title: Tracked generated .context artifacts (graph.json etc.) make every rebase conflict-heavy — 84 conflict markers in one file
evidence:
3 markers : .context/features/INDEX.json
3 markers : .context/features/INDEX.md
3 markers : .context/features/brand-hub/feature.json
6 markers : .context/features/brand-hub/spec.md
84 markers : .context/features/graph.json
3 markers : .context/meta.json
what_happened: The PR rebase onto development conflicted in two separate commits almost entirely inside .context/ (meta.json, INDEX.json/md, graph.json, symbol-graph.json, naming.md, source-docs INDEX, spec.md, feature.json). Claude spent the bulk of the session resolving machine-generated JSON conflicts (strategy: take --ours then regenerate), and curated spec.md/feature.json needed careful manual three-way merging. This is recurring overhead for any branch that touches code (drift commits are committed per the workflow).
root_cause_guess: Large deterministic JSON artifacts are committed to git with no merge driver (.gitattributes union/ours strategy) or 'regenerate on merge' tooling, so dummyindex's own commit-the-index discipline guarantees rebase conflicts.

title: context rebuild --changed destroys curated feature taxonomy, re-shattering it into ~100 community-N stubs
evidence: "One self-inflicted incident, fully reverted: I ran `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` per the plan and it re-shattered the curated `.context` index into ~100 auto `community-N` stubs (the known 0.25.0 bug). I hard-reset that commit, restored the curated index, scrubbed the rebuild step from the proposal, and saved a memory note so it never gets run again" — plus git stat showing `.context/features/community-0/feature.json | 1301 +` and stub text "# Feature: community-99 _Deterministic stub (`confidence: ConfidenceLevel.EXTRACTED`)..."
what_happened: The plan's own Wave-1 step prescribed `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` to fix a stale index. Running it (L407-414) replaced the curated features (brand-hub, chat, ...) with ~100 auto-clustered community-N stubs ("0 added, 6 modified, 16 removed" was misleading — the commit added thousands of stub lines). The assistant had to `git reset --hard HEAD~1` ("Drop the index-shattering rebuild commit"), edit the proposal/checklist to say "Do NOT run rebuild --changed", commit a warning, and write a memory note. The memory note states the frontend had ALREADY reverted its auto-rebuild hooks for this exact bug (commit 4477cef6), and that CLAUDE.md guidance recommending rebuild --changed is stale — so this is a recurring, documented-but-unfixed regression that the tool's own docs and generated plans keep prescribing.
root_cause_guess: 0.25.0 deterministic backbone rebuild no longer respects/preserves the curated (reconciled) taxonomy — it re-runs community clustering from scratch over the AST instead of mapping changed files into existing curated features; shipped CLAUDE.md/skill docs and the plan generator still recommend the destructive command.

title: Stale .context index: propose found '(none detected)' related features, and the only documented refresh path is the destructive rebuild
evidence: CLI: "context propose: .../art-direction-paper-rebuild (4 files) related features: (none detected)" — assistant summary: "Related features: the deterministic scan found none — because the `.context/` index predates the as-built art-direction page (none of its components are in `symbols.json`). The real related feature is `brand-hub`; the plan's Wave 1 includes `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` to fix the stale index."
what_happened: The deterministic related-feature retrieval that grounds proposals returned nothing for a page that plainly belongs to the existing brand-hub feature, because symbols.json predated the as-built code. The prescribed remedy — rebuild --changed — is the command that destroys the curated index (issue 1), and reconcile only runs at build close, leaving no safe way to refresh the backbone mid-flight. The second propose (after some commits) did detect brand-hub et al.
root_cause_guess: Index staleness + a catch-22 in the update model: backbone refresh is destructive and curation-preserving reconcile is only wired into the build-close procedure, so plan-time grounding silently degrades on drifted repos.

title: `rebuild --changed` re-runs AST extraction over all 1086 files when only 11 changed
evidence:
=== run rebuild --changed (refresh backbone for 11 changed files) ===
  AST extraction: 100/1086 files (9%)
  AST extraction: 200/1086 files (18%)
  ...
  AST extraction: 1086/1086 files (100%)
context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster).
what_happened: Claude invoked `rebuild --changed` expecting an incremental refresh for the 11 files changed since the anchor, but the progress output shows a full-repo AST extraction pass (1086/1086). The result was correct (only 6 .context files changed, enrichment preserved), but the --changed flag does not limit extraction work, making the 'incremental' path scale with repo size.
root_cause_guess: rebuild --changed only scopes which artefacts are rewritten, not the AST extraction phase — there's no extraction cache or changed-file filter feeding the extractor, so --changed is incremental in name only.

title: dummyindex indexes its own installed .claude/ tooling, flooding reconcile with self-generated 'unassigned new files'
evidence: L138 reconcile --json: "\"unassigned_new_files\": [\".claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/SKILL.md\", \".claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/agents/architecture.md\", ... \".claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/db-specialist-agent.md.tmpl\", ... \".claude/skills/dummyindex/council/65-reconcile.md\"]" — all 14 entries are dummyindex's own installer output. L123: "_New/unenriched code is a commit-anchored signal — it clears only when you reconcile."
what_happened: Every file the 0.19.2 installer itself wrote into `.claude/skills/` showed up as drift the council is told to 'place' into the feature taxonomy. Claude verified no feature owns `.claude` paths (L141-142) and told the user the remaining noise "still settle[s] with a `reconcile-stamp --force` after you commit" — manual churn caused entirely by the tool's own artifacts.
root_cause_guess: The file scanner has no default exclusion for `.claude/**` (or for paths the installer itself just wrote), so installer output is treated as project source needing curation.

title: Upgrade leaves stale 0.15-era artifact `.context/_enrich_plan.json` (392KB) at the index root; no migration/cleanup
evidence: L147: "-rwxr-xr-x 1 ahmed ahmed 392603 Jun  7 22:45 .context/_enrich_plan.json" (cache copy absent: "ls: cannot access '.context/cache/_enrich_plan.json': No such file or directory"). L183: "removed the stale 0.15-era `.context/_enrich_plan.json` (0.19 writes it under `cache/`)".
what_happened: After rebuild/refresh under 0.19.2, the obsolete 0.15 enrich-plan file remained at the `.context/` root (0.19 relocated it under `cache/`). Claude had to discover this by listing mtimes and manually `rm` it (L154).
root_cause_guess: rebuild/refresh only writes current-schema artifacts; there is no migration step or orphan-file sweep when the artifact layout changes between versions.

title: Known destructive behavior on record: full `rebuild` re-clusters into community-N and drops curated features
evidence: L177 (project MEMORY.md surfaced in-session): "- [Full rebuild destroys curated features](full-rebuild-destroys-curated-features.md) — dummyindex full `rebuild` re-clusters into community-N, drops curated semantic features; use `--changed` or recover via git."
what_happened: Not re-triggered in this session, but this prior-session lesson was loaded as project memory and actively shaped Claude's behavior (it exclusively used `--changed` and avoided ingest). It also makes reconcile's 're-run a fresh ingest' advice (L108) dangerous. Recorded here because the workaround burden persisted into this session.
root_cause_guess: Plain `rebuild` defaults to destructive re-clustering with no confirmation or curated-content guard; safe incremental mode is opt-in via `--changed` instead of the default.

title: .context/meta.json version stamp can only move via a destructive --full rebuild, so it permanently lies after upgrades
evidence: === rebuild --changed === ... context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster). ... === meta version after === "dummyindex_version": "0.15.0" ... assistant: '`meta.json` still stamps `dummyindex_version: 0.15.0`... `rebuild --changed` deliberately preserves it; bumping it to 0.24 would require a `--full` rebuild, which **re-clusters and destroys the curated features**. Not worth it.'
what_happened: After upgrading the CLI to 0.24.0 and running the documented non-destructive refresh, meta.json (which was modified by the rebuild) still claims the index was built by 0.15.0. The only sanctioned way to update the stamp destroys curated taxonomy, so the user must accept a permanently false provenance stamp — defeating the stamp's purpose and any future tooling that keys off it.
root_cause_guess: rebuild --changed rewrites meta.json counts but intentionally(?) skips dummyindex_version/schema_version; there is no migration/stamp-bump path decoupled from full re-cluster.

title: `rebuild --changed` says 'enriched index preserved' while simultaneously reporting removal of curated feature flow docs
evidence: context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster). ... removed files: .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-057.json, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-057.md, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-058.json, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-058.md, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-061.json, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-061.md
what_happened: The non-destructive refresh printed contradictory output — 'enriched index preserved' next to a 'removed files' list naming six curated flows/* docs inside a feature directory (flows/*.md are explicitly listed by the drift report as curated docs to maintain). Claude had to run a follow-up git audit (grep for spec.md/plan.md/concerns.md/feature.json changes) to convince itself and the user nothing curated was clobbered; the flows ambiguity was never fully explained.
root_cause_guess: rebuild prunes flow docs whose anchor symbols/files disappeared (the audit refactor split files), but reports it in the same breath as 'preserved' with no distinction between deterministic artefacts and council-enriched flow docs.

title: Giant generated .context diffs (33k+/24k lines) make commits unreviewable and fed the user's branch-state confusion
evidence: [development b5279b3] chore: update dummyindex to 0.24.0 + refresh .context backbone 10 files changed, 32841 insertions(+), 24391 deletions(-) ... assistant: 'One heads-up for whoever reviews #16: the diff is large by line count (~33k/24k) because the regenerated `.context/map/symbols.json` and `symbol-graph.json` are big generated JSON — it's deterministic output, not hand edits.'
what_happened: A routine backbone refresh produced a 57k-line diff needing a reviewer disclaimer. Earlier in the session the user was confused ('i checkout to development are all the commits in all the branches and work trees on to dev?' / 'files looked reverted'): the local-vs-origin development divergence was 'almost entirely .context/ + lockfile' from scattered .context commits (chore/updated-context branch, PR #12 'Chore/updated context', a local-only feat(audit) commit). dummyindex's heavyweight generated JSON in git is a recurring source of branch noise and review pain.
root_cause_guess: map/symbols.json and symbol-graph.json are monolithic regenerated files with unstable ordering/content; no diff-friendly sharding, .gitattributes guidance, or generated-file marking.

title: Completed/superseded proposals linger in .context/proposals/ with no lifecycle, forcing the user to ask plan to 'confirm the build'
evidence: Assistant (L27): "Substantial WIP MCP code already exists, plus an `mcp-server` feature in `.context/features/` and two proposals (`mcp-server`, `mcp-design-tokens-from-db`). Let me read what's already there before deciding what the proposal should be." User (L8): "i need you to confirm the mcp feature build, so far the spec and the plan was to port it from the old repo but now it has extended"
what_happened: The mcp-server feature is already built and indexed under .context/features/mcp-server/, yet its originating proposal still sits live in .context/proposals/mcp-server/, alongside a second overlapping proposal (mcp-design-tokens-from-db) whose spec says it 'reverses MCP Decision 2'. The user had to ask the plan skill to 'confirm the mcp feature build', and Claude had to spend the whole session untangling which of three artifacts (built feature doc, stale port proposal, decision-reversal proposal) reflected reality before it could plan the extension. One occurrence, but it shaped the entire session.
root_cause_guess: No proposal lifecycle in dummyindex (no archived/done/superseded status, no closure when build completes), and the plan skill documents only create-new (`--force` overwrite) — no extend/amend-existing-proposal verb.

title: v0.25.0 `rebuild --changed` run from hooks 're-shatters the curated index' — auto-refresh hooks had to be reverted
evidence:
4477cef6 revert(claude): drop auto rebuild --changed hooks — 0.25.0 re-shatters the curated index
29d029b3 chore(claude): sync dummyindex skill to 0.25.0
...
e5b91405 chore(claude): auto-keep .context current — LLM recouncil + deterministic refresh
what_happened: The frontend submodule's recent git history (read during this session to decide where to commit) shows the team built auto-refresh hooks around `rebuild --changed`, then after upgrading to 0.25.0 had to revert them because the rebuild destroyed the curated taxonomy — directly contradicting the CLI help shown this same session: '--changed preserves the taxonomy + enrichment and only refreshes deterministic artefacts'. The user's original ask ('after each pass it should auto triggrer update for relevant .context') is exactly the capability this regression killed.
root_cause_guess: Regression in 0.25.0's incremental rebuild: the curated/enriched detection fails (or hook execution context differs), so --changed falls through to the destructive re-cluster path despite documentation.

title: Known cosmetic index inaccuracy carried across sessions: INDEX confidence column reads EXTRACTED while docs are INFERRED
evidence: SessionStart memory (now.md, injected into this session): "INDEX `confidence` column reads EXTRACTED (cosmetic; spec/plan carry INFERRED)."
what_happened: The session-memory handoff injected at SessionStart explicitly flags that the INDEX.md confidence column shows EXTRACTED even though the feature spec.md/plan.md carry `confidence: INFERRED` (both brand-hub docs read in this session indeed show "`confidence: INFERRED`"). The mismatch survived the previous session's full build and this session's rebuild --changed — nothing fixed it.
root_cause_guess: INDEX.md regeneration derives confidence from feature.json's deterministic field instead of the enriched spec/plan confidence markers, and no reconcile/rebuild step syncs them.

title: SessionStart drift report flags dummyindex's own equip-generated agent file as unreconciled drift
evidence:
## .context/ drift report

### New files not yet in any feature

1 file(s) added since the index was last reconciled are owned by no feature. The reconcile procedure decides where each belongs (a new feature, or attached to an existing one).

- `.claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md`

_New/unenriched code is a commit-anchored signal — it clears only when you reconcile. Run `/dummyindex --recouncil` (see `council/65-reconcile.md`): place new files, (re-)enrich, then `reconcile-stamp` the anchor._
what_happened: At session start the drift hook reported drift whose only content was an untracked `.claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md` — a specialist agent file produced by dummyindex's own equip workflow, not application source. The report tells the user to run the full reconcile procedure (place into a feature, re-enrich, reconcile-stamp) just to clear a generated toolkit file. The prior session's handoff had ended with `has_drift: False`, so equip output created fresh 'drift' immediately. Seen once in this session (every new session in this repo would re-show it until reconciled).
root_cause_guess: The drift/new-file scanner has no exclusion for dummyindex-managed paths (`.claude/agents/`, `.claude/skills/`, `.context/` itself). Equip-generated artifacts should be claimed by equipment.json / hash baseline or ignored by the commit-anchored new-file detector, not routed into the feature-placement reconcile flow.

title: dummyindex-generated .context/map/files.json sha256 hashes trip detect-secrets and fail CI (~140 hits)
evidence:
ERROR: Potential secrets about to be committed to git repo!

Secret Type: Hex High Entropy String
Location:    .context/map/files.json:11

Secret Type: Hex High Entropy String
Location:    .context/map/files.json:20
[... ~140 more files.json hits ...]
Secret Type: Hex High Entropy String
Location:    .context/source-docs/INDEX.json:41

Secret Type: Hex High Entropy String
Location:    .context/source-docs/INDEX.json:98
what_happened: User pasted a failing GitHub Actions pre-commit run (`uv run pre-commit run --all-files --config .pre-commit-config-ci.yaml`). The detect-secrets hook flagged roughly 140 'Hex High Entropy String' locations, virtually all in committed dummyindex output: .context/map/files.json (per-file `"sha256": "a4c3095ab01..."` content hashes) and .context/source-docs/INDEX.json. The assistant verified they were dummyindex's own content hashes, not secrets, then had to exclude the whole .context/ tree from the hook. The hook also looped on '.secrets.baseline is unstaged' until the refreshed baseline was staged.
root_cause_guess: dummyindex embeds high-entropy sha256 content hashes in committed JSON artifacts and neither documents nor wires any detect-secrets exemption (baseline entry, pragma, or exclude) when it installs into a repo that has secret scanning. Since hashes regenerate on every rebuild, baselining is futile — the generator should emit scanner-safe output or the installer should add the exclude itself.

title: ~40 generated .context/ files fail trailing-whitespace and end-of-file pre-commit hooks
evidence:
trim trailing whitespace.................................................Failed
- hook id: trailing-whitespace
- exit code: 1

Fixing .claude/skills/dummyindex/council/10-structural-review.md
Fixing .context/proposals/mcp-design-tokens-from-db/plan.md

fix end of files.........................................................Failed
- hook id: end-of-file-fixer

Fixing .context/features/symbol-graph.json
Fixing .context/features/llm-uploads/_reality-check.md
Fixing .context/features/mcp-server/_reality-check.md
[...]
Fixing .claude/skills/dummyindex/.dummyindex_version
Fixing .context/features/INDEX.md
Fixing .context/features/mcp-server/feature.json
what_happened: The same CI run failed two basic hygiene hooks because ~40 committed dummyindex artifacts (_reality-check.md, docs.md, feature.json, INDEX.json/md, flow-*.md, symbol-graph.json, proposal plan.md, plus the vendored skill's council file and .dummyindex_version) have trailing whitespace or lack a final newline. Because the files are regenerated wholesale, one-time fixes would regress on the next rebuild, so the assistant excluded the trees from pre-commit instead of fixing the files.
root_cause_guess: dummyindex's markdown/JSON writers don't normalize output to common lint conventions (strip trailing whitespace, ensure POSIX final newline). Any consumer repo with standard pre-commit-hooks gets red CI the moment .context/ is committed.

title: Committed index artifacts churn massively — symbol-graph.json diff of 113,596 lines between consecutive days
evidence:
.context/features/symbol-graph.json | 113596 +++++++++---------
 .context/map/files.json            |    372 +-
 .context/map/symbols.json          |   4214 +-
[...]
21 files changed, 58781 insertions(+), 60735 deletions(-)
what_happened: Comparing a June 9 stash to the June 10 branch tip showed the committed .context/ backbone rewrote ~59k lines, dominated by symbol-graph.json (113,596-line diff) and map/symbols.json (4,214 lines). This made the lost-work audit slow, bloats every commit that includes a rebuild, and is the same wholesale-regeneration property that forced the pre-commit excludes ('regenerated wholesale, so cosmetic and entropy hooks churn on them endlessly').
root_cause_guess: Deterministic rebuild rewrites giant monolithic JSON artifacts (likely with unstable ordering or full re-serialization) rather than producing minimal stable diffs; committing these to git makes history and review unusable around index updates.

title: Drift accumulated to 66 source files across 12 features; SessionStart report ignored for the whole session
evidence:
## .context/ drift report

66 source files across 12 features have been edited since the matching `.context/features/<id>/` docs were last touched. If your current task overlaps any of these features, review and update the relevant docs (`spec.md`, `plan.md`, `concerns.md`, `flows/*.md`)...

_Drift clears naturally: once you edit a feature doc, its mtime updates and these entries drop off._
what_happened: The SessionStart hook ran fine and reported very large drift (66 files / 12 of the repo's features, including mcp-server and integrations-oauth which this session then touched via tests/core/mcp edits). The session never updated any .context/ doc, never ran rebuild/reconcile, and the drift only grew. Nothing in the workflow nudged again after the opening banner (Stop hook fired 3x with level='suggestion', no output, never surfaced drift).
root_cause_guess: Report-only drift with no actionable follow-through: the wall-of-text banner at session start is easy to ignore mid-task, and the mtime-based 'drift clears naturally' framing invites touching docs rather than reconciling content. (The later v0.23.0 reconcile-gate addresses this; this session predates it and shows exactly why it was needed.)

title: Auto-clustering produced 734 unusable Leiden communities; rebuild/ingest can re-shatter the hand-curated 13-feature taxonomy
evidence: collapsed 734 Leiden communities into the **13-feature taxonomy** via `scaffold-feature` ... "The `.context/` index uses a **13-feature taxonomy** ... hand-collapsed from 734 raw Leiden communities by file path. Features were built via `scaffold-feature` from path-based file lists, so they carry no auto-detected flows. If a future `ingest`/`rebuild` re-shatters the repo into `community-N` clusters, re-apply this regrouping rather than councilling the raw communities."
what_happened: The handoff/core-memories injected at SessionStart show the deterministic ingest produced 734 raw community-N clusters for this frontend repo — so unusable the user had to hand-collapse them into 13 features by file path. Worse, the user had to write a standing defensive memory warning future sessions that a routine `ingest`/`rebuild` may destroy that curation and re-shatter the taxonomy, and that path-scaffolded features 'carry no auto-detected flows'.
root_cause_guess: The Leiden community-detection backbone over-fragments large frontend codebases and has no mechanism to persist or respect a user-curated feature taxonomy across rebuilds — rebuild treats clustering output as authoritative instead of reconciling into the existing curated grouping.

title: No foreign-.context ownership guard: preflight unconditionally claims `.context/**` as managed
evidence: /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/context/domains/preflight/render.py: "_MANAGED_PATHS = (\".context/**\", \".claude/CLAUDE.md  (managed block only — surrounding content preserved)\", \".claude/settings.json  (one additive SessionStart hook)\")" — rendered under "## Will write / manage". No code path in preflight inventories or warns about an existing `.context/` that lacks `meta.json`.
what_happened: Latent hazard made concrete by this repo: if anyone runs `/dummyindex` at BOS-Mono root (plausible, given the global directive in issue 1 points there), preflight would announce it will 'write / manage' `.context/**` — which at this root means the project's curated docs tree and team memory store — with no warning that the directory belongs to another system. Git log confirms it has not fired yet (no dummyindex writes ever landed in the root .context/), but the live state is one invocation away from polluting a tracked docs tree.
root_cause_guess: Preflight inventories .claude/ (settings validity, rules, agents, CLAUDE.md block) and git cleanliness, but never reads `.context/meta.json` to verify ownership before promising to manage the tree. Needs a 'foreign .context detected (no meta.json) — refuse or require --force/alternate dir' check.

title: Tree enrichment (Phase 4.5) wiped by re-ingest: all 1117 tree.json abstracts are EXTRACTED stubs in a standard-mode repo
evidence: Live .context/tree.json: Counter({'EXTRACTED': 1117}) — every node abstract is a stub like 'Directory at app/ (6 immediate children).' Git history: `git show 6d7c31f:.context/tree.json | grep -c INFERRED` = 196; next commit touching it, 6a4cab6 'WIP: MCP port with dummyIndex', = 0. .context/config.json has "mode": "standard".
what_happened: Phase 4.5 enrichment originally ran (196 INFERRED node abstracts committed). A later ingest/rebuild regenerated tree.json and destroyed all enriched abstracts; enrichment was never re-run. The skill promises standard mode enriches the structure batch so 'a future session's PageIndex walk over tree.json reads real prose' — live retrieval reads only stubs, and nothing (drift hook, reconcile-gate, status) flags the loss. This also contradicts the dummyindex-update skill's 'NON-DESTRUCTIVE deterministic backbone rebuild — never touching curated .context taxonomy' framing.
root_cause_guess: tree.json is treated as deterministic backbone, so ingest/rebuild regenerates it wholesale instead of preserving INFERRED abstracts for unchanged nodes; no post-rebuild check reports enrichment regression.

title: Committed .context/config.json carries a machine-absolute external_docs path reaching outside the submodule
evidence: .context/config.json: '"external_docs": ["/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.context/docs"]' (committed; changed in submodule commits). .context/meta.json similarly commits '"root": "/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/backend"'.
what_happened: The onboarding-persisted external docs root is an absolute path on this machine pointing at the parent monorepo's .context/docs. Any other clone/CI/teammate of the backend submodule gets a dangling external_docs reference (and a wrong meta.json root) until they re-onboard. For a submodule the reference should be relative (e.g. ../.context/docs) or declared unresolvable-portably.
root_cause_guess: onboard/ingest persists user-supplied paths verbatim with no relativization or portability warning for paths outside the indexed root.

title: 0.25.0 install/rebuild re-shattered the curated 14-feature taxonomy into 838 phantom community-N features (committed)
evidence: frontend/.context/features/INDEX.json (HEAD): 838 features, first feature_id 'community-0'; 0 of the 838 listed dirs exist on disk, while all 14 real curated dirs (account, app-shell, auth, brain, brand-hub, chat, design-system-base, home, home-v3, projects, shared-lib, shared-ui-custom, tests, tooling) are absent from the index. Commit 4477cef6: "revert(claude): drop auto rebuild --changed hooks — 0.25.0 re-shatters the curated index ... `rebuild --changed` re-clusters the repo into ~838 raw community-N features instead of preserving the curated 13-feature taxonomy (0.24.0 preserved it)". HEAD 0509e767: "0.25.0 re-shatters curated index (confirmed live)".
what_happened: Commit 29d029b3 ('chore(claude): sync dummyindex skill to 0.25.0', via `dummyindex install`) replaced the curated features/INDEX.json (14 features at 29d029b3~1) with 838 raw Leiden 'community-N' stubs and committed it. The curated feature DIRS survived on disk but the index/INDEX.md/graph artifacts no longer reference them, so every retrieval path the docs prescribe ('Start at features/INDEX.json') routes agents into 838 nonexistent dirs. .context/INDEX.md 'Files in this run' lists features/community-0/docs.md etc. — none exist. .context/session-memory/core-memories.md had pre-warned: 'If a future ingest/rebuild re-shatters the repo into community-N clusters, re-apply this regrouping'. The user had to revert auto-rebuild hooks (4477cef6) and strip `rebuild --changed` from proposal preflight (0509e767) to defend the index — twice.
root_cause_guess: 0.25.0's rebuild/install path runs a full deterministic re-cluster (or a version-migration ingest) instead of preserving the existing features/ taxonomy on disk; the dummyindex-update skill promises a 'NON-DESTRUCTIVE deterministic backbone rebuild — never touching curated .context/ taxonomy' but the 0.25.0 implementation regenerates features/INDEX.json from raw graph communities, ignoring curated dirs created via scaffold-feature/features-rename.

title: meta.json stamps stale/misleading after the 0.25.0 churn: updated_at predates the latest regeneration and created_at was reset by the reinstall
evidence: .context/meta.json: {"created_at": "2026-06-12T19:17:42+00:00", "updated_at": "2026-06-12T19:34:56+00:00", "indexed_commit": "1b7fa81f...", "dummyindex_version": "0.25.0"} while HOW_TO_USE.md/INDEX.md/tree.json/meta.json mtimes are Jun 13 02:57–02:58 IST (~21:27 UTC Jun 12) — about two hours after updated_at.
what_happened: The 02:57 'confirmed live' rebuild rewrote the backbone artifacts (and touched meta.json) without bumping updated_at, so the freshness stamp under-reports the last regeneration. created_at (2026-06-12T19:17) coincides with the 0.25.0 install at 00:46 IST Jun 13 — the 'non-destructive' upgrade reset the index's birth date, erasing ancestry back to the original ingest. Version stamps themselves are consistent (CLI 0.25.0 = skill family 0.25.0 = meta dummyindex_version 0.25.0), and indexed_commit is a valid ancestor of HEAD as the Model B anchor design intends.
root_cause_guess: rebuild --changed rewrites meta.json fields selectively (or not at all) while regenerating siblings, and the install-triggered re-ingest recreates meta.json from scratch instead of preserving created_at.

title: rebuild --changed reports 'no feature drift detected' and skips feature regeneration after a sweeping domain migration, yet checklist items claiming regeneration get ticked
evidence: 'context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster). no feature drift detected.' — vs checklist item: 'dummyindex context rebuild --changed run; .context/features/* regenerated to the brand-centric reality.'
what_happened: After a migration touching ~15 backend files, rebuild --changed refreshed only 7 deterministic artefacts and claimed no feature drift. Checklist items asserting features were 'regenerated' were ticked anyway — index left stale while marked done.
root_cause_guess: Drift detection blind to semantic change inside already-indexed files; proposal generator writes acceptance text rebuild --changed cannot satisfy — only reconcile can, which was skipped.
